title: TC001-Log in as Admin and reach the dashboard
description: Verifies an Admin can log in through the Admin portal mode and is redirected to the dashboard on success.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5174
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174")
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (http://localhost:5174/login) to attempt to load the login UI so I can select Admin mode and complete the login form.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174/login")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/dashboard' in current_url, "The page should have navigated to /dashboard after a successful admin login."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The login UI could not be reached because the SPA did not load, preventing the test from running.

Observations:
- Navigated to http://localhost:5174/login and saw a blank/dark page with no interactive elements.
- Waiting on the page did not reveal any controls; the app shows 0 interactive elements.

title: TC002-Protected route redirects unauthenticated users to login

async def run_test():

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow

        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5174/dashboard and observe whether an unauthenticated user is redirected to the login page or shown a login form.

        # --> Assertions to verify final state

from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():

    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode

            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5174
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174")
        
        # -> Navigate to /login so the login form can be used to authenticate.

        # -> Try a different navigation approach: load the app root (http://localhost:5174) to see if the SPA renders there, then wait briefly and re-evaluate interactive elements.

        # --> Assertions to verify final state

        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Group')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The session details should show the session group, date, and status after opening a session"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:

        if browser:

title: TC015-Prevent group creation when frequency conflicts with schedules
description: An engineer cannot create a group when the selected frequency does not match the configured schedules and is shown a validation error.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5174
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174")
        
        # -> Load the app (or /login) so interactive elements appear and then proceed to sign in.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174/login")
        
        # -> Reload the application root so the SPA can finish loading and interactive elements (login form) appear; then proceed to login.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'The number of weekly schedules must match the selected frequency.')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The form should display a schedule or frequency validation error after submitting mismatched frequency and schedules."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
